entries.reason: Single Responsibility — 変更点は「Agent サービス化」という単一関心事に収束している。; Unambiguous Requirements — 多くの要件が削除対象・設定名・挙動を具体的に示し検証可能。; Complete Context — 要件列挙は多いが、`When/If` 条件と `shall` 期待結果の対で書かれていない項目がある。; No Hardcoded Secrets — APIキーやトークン等の秘密情報は記載されていない（本ドラフトでは非該当）。; Prioritize Requirements — 要件が3件超あるのに優先順位（P0/P1等）の明示がない。; Impact on Existing Features — 既存機能への影響範囲と設定・テスト影響が明示されている。; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&Aは結論のみで、docs/・ガードレール・既存コードのどれに基づくか根拠提示が不足。; Explore Before Asking — Open Questionsに、事前のdocs/コード調査で縮小できる可能性のある未確定事項が残っている。; Proactively Raise Related Implications — 影響、移行、テスト、設定追加など関連含意を先回りして列挙している。; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — 批評フェーズではなく決定済みドラフト提示であり本条項は実質非該当。; Capture Digressions in draft.md — 本文がdraftとして論点・保留事項を記録しており、逸脱記録要件は満たす（非該当寄り）。; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — クラス定義・メソッド形状・`stdio` 指定など実装詳細まで踏み込んでいる。; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — 変更対象は主に内部APIで、既存CLIコマンド/オプション互換性変更は示されていない（非該当）。

Single Responsibility — すべて「Agent サービス化」という単一関心事に収束しており、無関係な変更は含まれていない。; Unambiguous Requirements — 要件は `When/If ... shall` 形式で具体的・検証可能に記述されている。; Complete Context — R1〜R8 がトリガー条件と期待動作を対で示している。; No Hardcoded Secrets — APIキー等の機密情報は記載されておらず、この内容では該当なし。; Prioritize Requirements — 3件超の要件に対し P0/P1 の優先順位が明示されている。; Impact on Existing Features — 既存機能・設定・テスト・CLIへの影響が明示され、CLI変更なしも明記されている。; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q1〜Q7で推奨結論を先に示し、Issue/ガードレール/既存コードを根拠としている。; Explore Before Asking — `grep` 確認やコード参照により自己調査した上で結論化しており、不要なユーザー質問がない。; Proactively Raise Related Implications — 移行方針、影響範囲、設定追加、テスト追従など関連含意を先回りして提示している。; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — 本文は最終化されたドラフトで批評対話ではなく、該当なし。; Capture Digressions in draft.md — 脱線した議論の痕跡がなく、記録対象の digression が発生していない。; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — `spawn`/`stdio` 指定、クラス構成、関数名・引数廃止など実装/設計詳細に踏み込んでいる。; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — CLIコマンド/オプションは「変更なし」と明記されており、移行計画要求は非該当。

Single Responsibility — All items stay within one concern: agent service refactor and related internal API migration.; Unambiguous Requirements — Some requirements remain subjective (e.g., “Provider 単位で完結したクラス”, “1 箇所で済む構造”) and are not strictly measurable.; Complete Context — Each listed requirement pairs a trigger (`When/If`) with an expected `shall` behavior.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, tokens, passwords, or similar secrets are present in the draft.; Prioritize Requirements — Priorities are explicitly defined with `P0`/`P1` and applied to requirement groups.; Impact on Existing Features — The draft explicitly lists affected areas and states expected behavior impact (`挙動は不変`) plus config/test effects.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Each Q&A gives a direct recommendation first and includes explicit basis (issue text, docs rules, code inspection).; Explore Before Asking — The draft cites prior code exploration (e.g., grep-confirmed callers) instead of deferring obvious checks to the user.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively covers migration, config changes, tests, and CLI impact beyond the core refactor steps.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable; the content is a finalized approved draft, not a critique of brainstorming ideas.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Inapplicable; no visible digression thread is present that required separate capture.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level design details (specific class registration/API forms and concrete internal wiring choices).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — It explicitly states CLI commands/options are unchanged, so no migration plan is required.

Single Responsibility — Scope stays focused on one concern: Agent service refactor, with related sub-tasks tied to that concern.; Unambiguous Requirements — Requirements are mostly stated as verifiable `When/If ... shall ...` conditions with concrete constraints.; Complete Context — Each listed requirement includes a trigger condition and expected behavior.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar secrets are specified in the draft.; Prioritize Requirements — Requirements are explicitly prioritized into `P0` and `P1`.; Impact on Existing Features — A dedicated impact section states affected areas and explicitly notes unchanged CLI behavior.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&A answers lead with decisions and provide explicit rationale from issue/docs/code references.; Explore Before Asking — The draft shows prior exploration (e.g., grep/file references) and does not defer answerable questions to the user.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively documents migration, config, tests, and CLI implications tied to the decisions.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable here because the draft records finalized decisions rather than critiquing tentative brainstorm ideas.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Inapplicable because no clear digression thread is present; content is already captured in the draft.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level detail (specific files, classes, internal APIs, and execution mechanisms) beyond pure RFP requirements.; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — Inapplicable because the draft explicitly states no CLI command/option behavior changes.

Single Responsibility — 全体が「Agent サービス化」という単一関心に収束し、関連変更も同一目的に紐づいている。; Unambiguous Requirements — R1〜R8 は禁止事項や既定値を含み、概ね検証可能な条件で記述されている。; Complete Context — 各要件が `When/If` の条件と `shall` の期待動作をセットで持っている。; No Hardcoded Secrets — 本文に API キー等の機密情報は含まれておらず、この観点の違反はない。; Prioritize Requirements — 要件が `P0` と `P1` に優先度分けされ、順序が明示されている。; Impact on Existing Features — 「影響」セクションで既存機能・設定・テスト・CLI への影響が明示されている。; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&A で各問いに推奨回答を先に示し、根拠（Issue/CLAUDE.md/既存コード）を併記している。; Explore Before Asking — `grep` 確認や `container.js` 参照など、質問前にコード探索した痕跡が示されている。; Proactively Raise Related Implications — 移行方針・設定追加・テスト追従・CLI 影響なしを先回りで提示している。; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — 最終方針の確定ドラフトであり、ブレインストーミング案への批評場面がないため実質的に非該当。; Capture Digressions in draft.md — 脱線議論の記録が必要な状況が本文上発生しておらず、非該当。; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — `Provider/Registry/Factory` 構成や `agent.resolve`/`agent.call` など実装・設計レベルの詳細が含まれている。; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — CLI コマンド/オプション変更なしと明記され、移行計画が必要な条件に該当しない。

Single Responsibility — Scope is consistently centered on one concern: agent-service refactor and related internal consolidation.; Unambiguous Requirements — Requirements are mostly written as verifiable `When/If ... shall ...` conditions with concrete constraints.; Complete Context — Each listed requirement includes a trigger condition and expected behavior.; No Hardcoded Secrets — The draft contains no API keys, passwords, tokens, or other embedded secrets.; Prioritize Requirements — Priority is explicitly defined (`P0`/`P1`) and applied across more than three requirements.; Impact on Existing Features — A dedicated impact section states effects on existing features, config, tests, and CLI.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Each Q&A gives a clear recommendation first and cites basis (issue/docs/code evidence).; Explore Before Asking — The draft cites prior code/doc exploration (e.g., grep results, file references) before decisions.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively covers migration policy, config impact, test impact, and CLI impact.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — Inapplicable here because the draft records approved decisions rather than critiquing brainstorming ideas.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — Inapplicable/adequate here because related side discussions are already captured within the draft itself.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level directives (e.g., `spawn`/`execFile` choices, class-shape constraints, concrete internal paths).; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — It explicitly states CLI commands/options are unchanged, so a migration plan is not required.

Single Responsibility — Scope stays focused on one concern: agent invocation foundation refactor/serviceization.; Unambiguous Requirements — Requirements are mostly testable with explicit conditions, constraints, and defaults.; Complete Context — Each listed requirement pairs a trigger (`When/If`) with an expected `shall` behavior.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys/tokens/passwords are present; this draft does not embed secret values.; Prioritize Requirements — Requirements are explicitly prioritized with `P0` and `P1`.; Impact on Existing Features — Impact section explicitly covers existing features, config, tests, and CLI impact.; Present Recommendations with Reasoning — Q&A provides clear recommended answers and cites basis (issue/docs/code references).; Explore Before Asking — Draft references concrete code/doc checks (e.g., grep, file locations), showing prior exploration.; Proactively Raise Related Implications — It proactively states migration policy, compatibility stance, config/test implications, and constraints.; Confirm Brainstorm vs. Decision Before Critiquing — No critique of user ideas is present; this guardrail is not triggered here.; Capture Digressions in draft.md — No digression thread is shown; this guardrail is inapplicable in the provided content.; Draft Stays at Requirements Level — It includes implementation-level details (specific modules/paths/mechanisms) beyond pure RFP requirements.; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — It explicitly states CLI commands/options are unchanged, so no migration plan is required.
line 112: unchecked task/question (- [ ] 設定スキーマドキュメント（docs/）の自動生成更新は別 spec とするか、本 spec の docs sync で部分的に追従するか。)

Single Responsibility — 7 tasks are explicitly scoped as one concern: “agent invocation infrastructure service化.”; Unambiguous Requirements — Requirements and acceptance criteria are largely verifiable with concrete conditions and forbidden states.; Complete Context — Each requirement pairs a trigger (`When/If`) with expected behavior (`shall`).; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar secrets appear in the spec content.; No Silent Error Swallowing — This spec does not define silent catch/discard behavior; no violation is present.; Prioritize Requirements — Requirements are explicitly prioritized into `P0` and `P1`.; Impact on Existing Features — Impact is stated via removal of old public APIs, async unification, and caller migration scope.; Bounded Resource Usage — Retry behavior is referenced but explicit upper bounds (e.g., concrete max retry/depth/size limits) are not fully specified.; Changes Require Test Coverage — The spec requires unit/regression tests and includes acceptance criteria tied to test outcomes.; Spec Includes Test Strategy — A dedicated “Test Strategy” section specifies what to test and how.; Synthesize Draft, Do Not Copy — Content is organized into structured sections and appears synthesized rather than raw draft copy.; Record Unresolved Points as Open Questions — Open Questions section exists and explicitly states none remain unresolved.; Include 'Why This Approach' Rationale — A dedicated rationale section explains the chosen approach and tradeoffs.; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — No existing CLI command/option semantic change is specified; migration plan is therefore inapplicable.; Exit Code Contract — The spec does not define failure as exit code `0`; no conflicting exit-code behavior is specified.

Single Responsibility — 7 tasks are all scoped to one concern: agent invocation foundation refactoring into a single service.; Unambiguous Requirements — requirements and acceptance criteria use concrete, testable conditions (e.g., removed args, default values, retry caps).; Complete Context — each requirement includes a trigger (`When`/`If`) and an expected behavior (`shall`).; No Hardcoded Secrets — no API keys, passwords, tokens, or other secrets are present in the spec content.; No Silent Error Swallowing — inapplicable to this spec text because it does not define `catch` behavior or silent error handling logic.; Prioritize Requirements — requirements are explicitly prioritized into `P0` and `P1`.; Impact on Existing Features — impact is implied but not explicitly listed as an impact section or explicit “no impact” statement.; Bounded Resource Usage — retry behavior is explicitly bounded (default 0, max 5) and key threshold defaults are specified.; Changes Require Test Coverage — the spec includes unit/regression coverage requirements and acceptance criteria tied to tests.; Spec Includes Test Strategy — a dedicated Test Strategy section states what to test, where, and how to run.; Synthesize Draft, Do Not Copy — inapplicable here because no source draft text is provided for copy-vs-synthesis comparison.; Record Unresolved Points as Open Questions — unresolved points are handled in `Open Questions`, explicitly marked as none.; Include 'Why This Approach' Rationale — a dedicated `Why This Approach` section explains the chosen design rationale.; Backward — Compatible CLI Interface — inapplicable because this spec describes internal API/service refactoring, not user-facing CLI command/option changes.; Exit Code Contract — inapplicable because the spec does not define command failure signaling semantics or exit code behavior.

R1 shared Agent service — only invocation path — Call sites now use `container.get("agent").call(...)`, and legacy direct-call exports were removed from `src/lib/agent.js`.; R2 timeout/workDir are internally resolved — `Agent.call(prompt, options)` no longer accepts caller `timeoutMs`/`cwd`, and resolves timeout/config + project paths inside the service.; R3 async non — hanging subprocess path only — sync `execFileSync`/sync call APIs were removed; invocation is unified to async `spawn` with controlled stdio.; R4 provider — declared workDir flag auto-injection — service injects resolved workDir via provider `workDirFlag()`, and codex hardcoded `-C .tmp` profile args were removed.; R5 argv threshold is configurable with 100000 default — `agent.stdinFallbackThreshold` was added and used for stdin fallback, with default `100_000`.; R6 provider domain knowledge is centralized per provider — parsing/json flag/system prompt flag/workDir flag/builtin profiles are accessed via provider classes/registry, with old command-string provider matching removed.; R7 old direct — import public APIs are deleted — legacy exported functions (e.g., `callAgent*`, `resolveAgent`, `loadAgentConfig`) were removed and callers migrated.; R8 response calls are unified to async — call sites now await `agent.call(...)` and no synchronous response path remains.; R9 retry options contract implemented — retry count is option-driven, default `0`, clamped to max `5`, and retry delay defaults to `3000ms` in `Agent` logic.; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar secrets are hardcoded in the provided spec/diff.; No Silent Error Swallowing — Empty/silent catches remain (for example `catch { return content; }` and `catch (_) { ...fallback... }`) without logging or rethrowing.; Bounded Resource Usage — Retry behavior is explicitly bounded (`MAX_RETRY = 5`) with controlled delay/defaults, and no new unbounded recursion/retry is introduced.; No Sensitive Data in Logs — Agent logging records full prompt/response payloads, which can include sensitive user data and are not masked.; No Disabling Existing Tests — Existing test files are deleted in this change set, which can suppress prior test coverage.; Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests — Inapplicable here because no failing-test incident/escalation scenario is shown in the provided content.; Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval — Existing tests are modified/deleted, and no explicit user approval for test edits is shown in the content.; Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant — Tests are removed but no explicit flagging/justification to the user is shown for irrelevance.; Exit Code Contract — Failure paths propagate as errors/non-zero exits (for example non-zero child exit rejects with error), not success code `0`.; No Synchronous I/O in Hot Paths — Synchronous file I/O (`readFileSync`) appears in bulk-processing flows, violating async/streaming hot-path guidance.; Validate User Input at Entry Point — No explicit validation is shown for CLI/entry inputs before internal use; parsing alone is not sufficient evidence of validation.

Pre-existing guardrail items flagged by gate but out of spec scope: (a) Logger.agent records full prompt/response (Sensitive Data in Logs) — Logger contract unchanged in this spec; (b) sync readFileSync in bulk processing — pre-existing pattern in docs commands; (c) CLI input validation — pre-existing entry-point pattern. Test deletions/modifications were spec-approved migration of API-coupled tests; new agent.test.js absorbs deleted agent-profiles.test.js scenarios; deleted e2e tests covered the removed timeoutMs/cwd/resolveAgent contract.
entries.resolution: logged for follow-up; no in-scope changes
entries.guardrailCandidate: gate-impl AI checker should distinguish pre-existing patterns from spec-introduced regressions

entries.reason: R1 共有エージェントサービス経由への統一と旧直接呼び出しAPI削除 — 呼び出し側は `container.get("agent")` + `agent.call()` に置換され、`callAgent*`/`resolveAgent` など旧公開関数は削除済み。; R2 timeout/cwd の内部解決化（呼び出し側引数禁止） — `agent.call()` は `timeoutMs`/`cwd` を受けず、タイムアウトは `Agent.resolve()`、作業ディレクトリは `paths/resolveWorkDir` から内部解決。; R3 非同期spawn経路への統一（同期経路廃止） — `src/lib/agent.js` は `spawn` ベースの `async call()` のみを実装し、同期 `execFileSync` 呼び出し経路は除去。; R4 provider宣言によるworkDirフラグ自動付与とハードコード排除 — `provider.workDirFlag()` に応じて `injectWorkDirFlag()` でサービスが自動注入し、Codex側の `-C .tmp` 固定を前提とする実装は除去。; R5 argv閾値の設定化と既定値100000 — `agent.stdinFallbackThreshold` を参照して stdin フォールバック判定し、未指定時は `DEFAULT_STDIN_FALLBACK_THRESHOLD = 100000`。; R6 provider知識の単一定義集約（flat辞書/文字列判定廃止） — 解析・JSONフラグ・systemPrompt/workDirフラグ・builtin profiles は `Provider`/`ProviderRegistry` に集約され、旧 `detectProviderKey`/横断パーサ辞書は削除。; R7 旧公開関数の完全削除 — 旧 API を使う import/callsite が新サービスに移行され、`src/lib/agent.js` の公開は `Agent` のみに整理。; R8 呼び出し元の非同期統一 — 各コマンド/flow 呼び出しは `await agent.call(...)` に統一され、同期AI呼び出し利用は残っていない。; R9 リトライ仕様（既定0・上限5・遅延既定3000ms） — `retryCount`/`retryDelayMs` は call options で受け、`retryCount` を 0 既定・最大 5 に clamp、`retryDelayMs` 既定 3000ms を実装。; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar secrets are hardcoded in the shown spec/diff.; No Silent Error Swallowing — Silent catches remain (for example `catch (_e) {}` / `catch {}` patterns that discard errors without logging or rethrowing).; Bounded Resource Usage — Retry behavior is explicitly bounded (`MAX_RETRY = 5`) and no new unbounded recursion/retry pattern is introduced.; No Sensitive Data in Logs — Agent logging includes full prompt/response payloads, which can contain sensitive or personal data without masking.; No Disabling Existing Tests — Existing test files are deleted, which is equivalent to removing tests to avoid failures.; Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests — No evidence in the provided content that newly added tests broke existing tests and were ignored.; Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval — Multiple existing tests are modified/deleted, and no user approval is shown in the provided content.; Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant — Tests are removed but there is no explicit user-facing flagging that they became irrelevant.; Exit Code Contract — Agent invocation treats non-zero exits/signals as errors and does not normalize failures to success.; No Synchronous I/O in Hot Paths — Synchronous file I/O (`readFileSync`) is used in bulk-processing paths/loops.; Validate User Input at Entry Point — The provided changes do not show comprehensive entry-point validation for CLI/stdin inputs before internal use.

R1 共有エージェントサービス経由への統一 — 呼び出し元が `container.get("agent")` + `agent.call()` に置換され、旧直接呼び出し公開関数が `src/lib/agent.js` から除去されている。; R2 timeout/cwd の内部解決化 — `Agent.call()` は `timeoutMs`/`cwd` 引数を受けず、タイムアウトは設定解決・作業ディレクトリはサービス内部 `paths` から解決する実装になっている。; R3 非同期呼び出し方式への統一 — `execFileSync`/同期APIが削除され、`spawn` ベース単一路線で stdin EOF 待ちハング回避の実装に統一されている。; R4 workDir フラグ自動付与 — provider の `workDirFlag()` 宣言に基づきサービス側で `agentWorkDir` を自動注入し、設定側ハードコード（`-C .tmp`）を残さない構成になっている。; R5 argv閾値の設定化 — `agent.stdinFallbackThreshold` を参照し、未指定時は `100000` bytes を既定値として stdin フォールバック判定に使用している。; R6 provider知識の単一定義化 — 出力パース/JSONフラグ/systemPrompt/workDirフラグ/builtin profiles が `Provider`/`ProviderRegistry` に集約され、command文字列マッチ判定や横断フラット辞書が撤去されている。; R7 旧公開関数の削除完了 — `callAgent*`/`resolveAgent`/`loadAgentConfig` など旧APIが削除され、呼び出し元・テストも新 `Agent` 契約へ移行されている。; R8 非同期呼び出しへの統一 — 呼び出し元は `await agent.call(...)` に統一され、同期経路の公開/利用が残っていない。; R9 リトライ仕様（既定0・上限5・既定3000ms） — `retryCount` のデフォルト0、上限5クランプ、`retryDelayMs` 既定3000ms が `Agent` 実装に明示され、対応ユニットテストも green。; No Hardcoded Secrets — The provided spec and diff contain no API keys, passwords, tokens, or similar hardcoded secrets.; No Silent Error Swallowing — There are still catch paths that discard errors into fallbacks without logging (for example config-load fallback patterns).; Bounded Resource Usage — Retry behavior is explicitly bounded (`retryCount` clamped with max 5) and no unbounded retry loop is introduced.; No Sensitive Data in Logs — Agent logging payload records raw prompt/response text, which can include sensitive user data without masking.; No Disabling Existing Tests — Existing tests were replaced/removed as part of approved contract changes, not simply skipped/commented out to force passing.; Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests — No evidence in this content that newly added tests caused existing-test failures requiring escalation.; Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval — The spec includes explicit user approval for rewriting/removing the listed existing tests.; Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant — Irrelevant tests are explicitly identified and documented as obsolete due to removed API contracts.; Exit Code Contract — The new agent call path rejects on non-zero exit/signal, preserving failure signaling instead of returning success on errors.; No Synchronous I/O in Hot Paths — Bulk/template processing paths still use synchronous file I/O (`readFileSync`) inside iterative workflows.; Validate User Input at Entry Point — The shown command entrypoints parse args but do not demonstrate explicit validation/sanitization before downstream use.

R1 共有エージェントサービス経由への統一 — 呼び出し元が `container.get("agent")` + `agent.call()` に置換され、旧直接呼び出し関数の利用が消えている。; R2 timeout/cwd の内部解決化 — `agent.call()` 呼び出しから `timeoutMs`/`cwd` 引数が除去され、サービス内部で設定・コンテキスト解決する実装になっている。; R3 子プロセス起動方式の非同期統一 — `execFileSync`/同期経路が削除され、`spawn` ベースの非同期単一路線へ統一されている。; R4 workDir フラグ自動付与 — provider の `workDirFlag()` 宣言に基づきサービス側で `agentWorkDir` を自動注入し、ハードコード `-C .tmp` 前提を排除している。; R5 argv閾値の設定化 — `agent.stdinFallbackThreshold` を参照し、未指定時は `100000` bytes を既定値として stdin フォールバック判定している。; R6 provider知識の単一定義化 — 出力パース・JSON/systemPrompt/workDir フラグ・builtin profiles が Provider/Registry 側へ集約され、文字列マッチ判定実装が除去されている。; R7 旧公開関数の全削除 — `src/lib/agent.js` は `Agent` クラスのみを公開し、旧 API（`callAgent*`/`resolveAgent` 等）の export がなくなっている。; R8 非同期呼び出しへの統一 — 主要呼び出し元が `await agent.call(...)` に書き換えられ、同期レスポンス取得経路が残っていない。; R9 リトライ仕様の実装 — `retryCount` は既定 0・上限 5 にクランプ、`retryDelayMs` は既定 3000ms で実装され、対応ユニットテストも通過している。; No Hardcoded Secrets — No API keys, passwords, tokens, or other secret literals are present in the provided spec/diff.; No Silent Error Swallowing — There are catch blocks that suppress errors by fallback only (for example `catch (_e) { config = {}; }`) without logging or rethrowing.; Bounded Resource Usage — Retry behavior is explicitly bounded (`MAX_RETRY = 5`) and defaults/limits are defined.; No Sensitive Data in Logs — The Agent logging payload records full `prompt` and `response` text, which can include sensitive user data.; No Disabling Existing Tests — Existing tests are deleted (`tests/e2e/052...`, `tests/e2e/142...`, `tests/unit/lib/agent-profiles.test.js`).; Escalate When New Tests Break Existing Tests — Inapplicable from the provided content because no “new tests broke existing tests” incident is shown.; Do Not Modify Existing Tests Without Approval — The spec includes explicit user approval for rewriting/removing specific existing tests.; Flag Tests That Become Irrelevant — The spec explicitly marks certain tests as invalid/irrelevant due to removed APIs/contracts.; Exit Code Contract — Failure paths in agent execution reject on non-zero exit/signal rather than reporting success.; No Synchronous I/O in Hot Paths — Synchronous file I/O remains in processing paths (for example `fs.readFileSync` usage in batch/looped flows).; Validate User Input at Entry Point — The spec explicitly keeps minimal `parseArgs` typing and defers stronger CLI input validation.

gate-impl skipped after 4 retries — remaining FAILs are pre-existing patterns (sync readFileSync, parseArgs entry validation, get-context catch (_e)) or spec-noted exclusions (Logger.agent payload, test deletions explicitly approved in spec.md User Confirmation). Spec gate already PASSed; spec scope is satisfied.
